title: fuel_shortage
description: The backup generator is running during an extended outage when a fuel delivery failure leaves tanks critically low.

timeline.title: Extended Outage — Generator Running
timeline.description: Generator has been running for 18 hours during an extended grid outage. Fuel tank at 40%.

timeline.title: Fuel Delivery Delayed
timeline.description: Contracted fuel supplier unable to deliver — regional shortage and prioritisation to hospitals.

timeline.title: Low Fuel Alert
timeline.description: Fuel tank drops to 20%. Estimated runtime at current load is 6 hours.

timeline.title: Emergency Fuel Sourcing
timeline.description: Alternative suppliers contacted. Load shedding initiated to extend runtime.

timeline.title: Fuel Arrives
timeline.description: Emergency fuel delivery secured from alternative supplier. Generator refuelled.

timeline.title: Grid Power Restored
timeline.description: Utility grid power restored. Generator safely shut down and fuel inventory reviewed.

node-1.title: Fuel Delivery Failure Confirmed
node-1.situation: The backup generator has been running for 18 hours during a regional grid outage. The fuel tank is at 40%. Your contracted fuel supplier has just informed you they cannot deliver due to a regional shortage — priority is being given to hospitals and emergency services. The utility company estimates grid restoration in 12–18 hours. At current load, your remaining fuel gives approximately 12 hours of runtime.
node-1.question: What is your immediate priority?

options.label: A
options.text: Immediately begin sourcing alternative fuel suppliers, assess which systems can be shed to reduce fuel consumption and extend runtime, and notify IT and facilities leadership of the developing situation.

options.label: B
options.text: Wait and see if the primary supplier can resolve the issue — you still have 12 hours of fuel.

options.label: C
options.text: Immediately shut down all non-essential systems regardless of business impact to maximise fuel runtime.

node-2.title: Load Reduction Planning
node-2.situation: The fuel tank is now at 30%. Three alternative fuel suppliers have been contacted — two cannot deliver until tomorrow and one can deliver in 4 hours but at a significant premium. At current load, remaining fuel lasts approximately 9 hours. If non-essential workloads are shut down, runtime could be extended to approximately 14 hours.
node-2.question: How do you manage the load and fuel cost decision?

options.label: A
options.text: Confirm the emergency fuel delivery from the available supplier despite the premium — continuity of critical operations justifies the cost. Simultaneously shed non-essential loads to maximise the safety margin.

options.label: B
options.text: Decline the expensive emergency supplier and bank on the primary supplier resolving the issue.

node-3.title: Critical Load Prioritisation
node-3.situation: Non-essential systems have been shut down and the emergency fuel delivery is confirmed for 3 hours from now. The fuel tank is at 20%, giving an estimated 6-hour runtime. The business has asked why the development and test environments have been shut down.
node-3.question: How do you communicate the load shedding decision?

options.label: A
options.text: Explain clearly that the load shedding is necessary to ensure critical production systems remain operational during a fuel shortage, and commit to restoring non-critical systems as soon as fuel levels are secured.

options.label: B
options.text: Restore the development environments to avoid business complaints — the remaining fuel should be sufficient.

node-4.title: Emergency Fuel Delivery
node-4.situation: The emergency fuel delivery has arrived. The delivery driver advises that refuelling must be done with the generator running — a hot refuel. This requires following specific hot refuel safety procedures. One facilities team member wants to refuel quickly without following the formal procedure to speed things up.
node-4.question: How do you manage the refuelling process?

options.label: A
options.text: Insist on following the hot refuel safety procedure in full — it includes fire extinguisher placement, no smoking enforcement, and a two-person supervised process. Speed must not compromise safety during a live generator refuel.

options.label: B
options.text: Allow the informal approach — the generator is familiar equipment and the procedure is largely precautionary.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: The generator has been safely refuelled following proper hot refuel procedures. Grid power was subsequently restored 6 hours later. The generator was safely shut down and fuel inventory replenished. A review of the fuel supply contract, on-site fuel storage capacity, and emergency supplier agreements has been commissioned.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
